# UC-14.3.11 · Greenhouse Humidity & Growth Optimization

## Description

Optimizes plant growth rates by maintaining ideal VPD (vapor pressure deficit) and reducing fungal disease risk.

## Value

Optimizes plant growth rates by maintaining ideal VPD (vapor pressure deficit) and reducing fungal disease risk.

## Implementation

Deploy Edge Hub with external humidity/temp probe via I²C (3.5mm jack) placed in plant canopy zone. Optional light sensor integration measures lux for photosynthesis optimization. Build custom ARM64 container to interface with greenhouse HVAC controller via Modbus TCP (port 502) for automated adjustment. Store 3M data points locally for real-time analytics without cloud latency.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Edge Hub (humidity + temperature + optional light sensor), custom edge.json container.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=edge-hub-data` metric_name=humidity OR metric_name=temperature OR metric_name=light, `sourcetype=edge_hub`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Deploy Edge Hub with external humidity/temp probe via I²C (3.5mm jack) placed in plant canopy zone. Optional light sensor integration measures lux for photosynthesis optimization. Build custom ARM64 container to interface with greenhouse HVAC controller via Modbus TCP (port 502) for automated adjustment. Store 3M data points locally for real-time analytics without cloud latency.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
| mstats avg(temperature) as temp, avg(humidity) as rh, max(light_level) as lux by host, _time span=1h
| eval sat_pressure=610.5*exp((17.27*temp)/(temp+237.7))
| eval vpd=(sat_pressure*(100-rh)/100)/1000
| eval growth_optimal=if(vpd>=0.8 AND vpd<=1.5 AND temp>=20 AND temp<=28, "YES", "NO")
| stats count(eval(growth_optimal="YES")) as optimal_hours, count as total_hours by host
| eval growth_score=(optimal_hours/total_hours)*100
```

Understanding this SPL

**Greenhouse Humidity & Growth Optimization** — Optimizes plant growth rates by maintaining ideal VPD (vapor pressure deficit) and reducing fungal disease risk.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=edge-hub-data` metric_name=humidity OR metric_name=temperature OR metric_name=light, `sourcetype=edge_hub`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Edge Hub (humidity + temperature + optional light sensor), custom edge.json container. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Uses `mstats` to query metrics indexes (pre-aggregated metric data).
• `eval` defines or adjusts **sat_pressure** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **vpd** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **growth_optimal** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by host** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **growth_score** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.


Note: for mstats, confirm the metrics indexes in the search exist and your role can run mstats. This is separate from CIM data model acceleration.

Step 3 — Validate
Confirm metric or event volume in the time range, then compare trends and device status to the Splunk Edge Hub admin UI or the mobile app for the same devices. Verify metric names, hosts, and time skew.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: VPD gauge, growth score trend, hourly optimization heatmap.

## SPL

```spl
| mstats avg(temperature) as temp, avg(humidity) as rh, max(light_level) as lux by host, _time span=1h
| eval sat_pressure=610.5*exp((17.27*temp)/(temp+237.7))
| eval vpd=(sat_pressure*(100-rh)/100)/1000
| eval growth_optimal=if(vpd>=0.8 AND vpd<=1.5 AND temp>=20 AND temp<=28, "YES", "NO")
| stats count(eval(growth_optimal="YES")) as optimal_hours, count as total_hours by host
| eval growth_score=(optimal_hours/total_hours)*100
```

## Visualization

VPD gauge, growth score trend, hourly optimization heatmap.
